- The green U-shape is below the white body
- One red foot is visible at the bottom of the white body (rows 41-43, cols 44-46)
- The green U-shape is at rows 29-49, cols 41-49
The character's white body is at rows 26-40, cols 35-49. The green U-shape is at rows 29-49, cols 41-49. So the character is overlapping with the green U-shape at rows 29-40, cols 41-49.
The red foot at rows 41-43, cols 44-46 is inside the green U-shape's hole (which is at rows 32-46, cols 44-46). This is interesting!
Maybe the goal is to have the character's red feet inside the green U-shape's hole. Currently, only one red foot is visible at rows 41-43, cols 44-46, which is inside the hole.
Let me think about this more carefully. The green U-shape's hole is at rows 32-46, cols 44-46 (3 wide, 15 high). The red foot is at rows 41-43, cols 44-46 (3 wide, 3 high). So the red foot is inside the hole.
Maybe the goal is to have all 4 red feet inside the green U-shape's hole. But the hole is only 3 wide and 15 high, and each red foot is 3x3. So 4 red feet would be 12 high, which fits inside the 15-high hole.
But the character's white body is 15 wide and 15 high, and the red feet are attached to the white body. So the character's shape determines where the red feet are positioned.
Let me think about the character's shape in the initial orientation:
- White body: "C" shape opening to the right
- Red feet: 4 blocks at the bottom of the white body

Actually, looking at the image, I see the white body is a "C" shape opening to the right. The green U-shape is below the white body. The red foot is at the bottom of the white body, inside the green U-shape's hole.
Let me try moving the character DOWN to see if more red feet become visible inside the green U-shape's hole.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the goal. Maybe the goal is to have the character's white body completely cover the green U-shape. Or maybe the goal is to have the character's white body match the green U-shape's shape exactly.
Looking at the image, the white body is a "C" shape opening to the right, and the green U-shape is a "U" shape opening upward. They don't match.
Let me try a different approach. Maybe the goal is to have the character's white body and red feet form a complete shape that matches the green U-shape. Or maybe the goal is to have the character's white body fit inside the green U-shape's hole.
Actually, I think I need to try moving the character DOWN to see if it completes the level. The character's white body is at rows 26-40, and the green U-shape is at rows 29-49. If I move the character DOWN, the white body will overlap more with the green U-shape.
